soap:
RE-REVIEW · lance-format/lance#6215 — feat: add manifest namespace extended properties
reviewed ed73cb73 → 95746654  (packet range spans 67 commits / base-branch churn; PR-authored delta = 1 commit, 3 source files)

PACKET INTEGRITY NOTE (read first)
  The deterministic packet is NOT trustworthy for this PR and was NOT used as the evidence base.
  - It reported the three authored source files (`Cargo.toml`, `dir.rs`, `dir/manifest.rs`) as additions:0 deletions:0 patch:null while emitting only Cargo.lock churn.
  - Root cause: `last_reviewed_sha` (ed73cb73) is NOT an ancestor of head (95746654) — the branch was rebased onto a new base (2a4daf13). The B..H range sweeps in 5 unrelated merged PRs and ~218 base files; the scoper kept the authored files but stripped their net patch to empty.
  - Ground truth used instead: GitHub PR file list (6 files, +989/−104, commits=1) + the single authored commit diffed directly. PR is currently mergeable=false (base conflicts).

CONDITIONS LEDGER (0/0 met)
  (empty) No prior conditions reconstructed — no posted issue(blocking) comments existed on the reviewed SHA. This re-review is effectively a first substantive pass over the authored delta.

FRESH-EYES DELTA
  The authored change adds "extended properties": property keys prefixed `lance.manifest.extended.` are stored as dedicated typed columns in the `__manifest` table; non-prefixed keys stay in the `metadata` JSON. Adds add/remove/list of extended columns, a `query_manifest`/`parse_manifest_object` refactor, and threads `extended_batch` through create/declare/register table + create namespace. Solid test coverage (8 new rstest cases). Findings:

  issue(non-blocking): silent data loss for legitimate string value "null".
    rust/lance-namespace-impls/src/dir/manifest.rs:720-722 (is_nullish_extended_value) treats both "" and any case of "null" as absent and skips writing the column. A user storing the literal string "null" in a Utf8 extended column has it silently dropped on write and absent on read. The empty-string carve-out is also asymmetric with scalar_to_str. Suggest gating the "null" sentinel on non-Utf8 types only, or documenting it as reserved.

  issue(non-blocking): API/shape mismatch between entries and extended batch.
    manifest.rs:303-323 — insert_into_manifest_with_metadata takes Vec<ManifestEntry> (N rows) but extended_batch is always exactly 1 row. batch.merge errors when num_rows differ (rust/lance-arrow/src/lib.rs:636). Works today only because every caller passes a single entry. Either assert entries.len()==1 when extended_batch.is_some(), or build the extended batch per-row.

  issue(non-blocking): read/modify schema race on extended columns.
    create/declare/register/create_namespace read extended_manifest_schema() then insert later; a concurrent remove_extended_properties dropping that column between read and insert would make the merge target a non-existent column. Matches the file's existing concurrency posture, so not newly regressed — flagging for awareness.

  nit: `use std::{ ..., f32, f64, ... }` (manifest.rs:47) imports the primitive modules unnecessarily.

  No new memory-safety, unsafe, panic-on-untrusted-input, or SQL-injection regressions found: object_id is escaped for the filter (manifest.rs:236, 329), and extended column writes go through typed ScalarValue conversion with explicit error variants.

RECOMMENDATION: comment (no blocking conditions)
  confidence: medium-high on the delta review; LOW on the packet — the deterministic packet was defective and bypassed. what would make this wrong: the prior-review history may have held blocking conditions I could not reconstruct (packet returned an empty ledger, and the rebased/non-ancestor reviewed SHA means I could not anchor a prior conditions thread). The attending should confirm no outstanding blocking thread exists before co-signing. Also note PR is mergeable=false (base conflicts).
